picks.game: Vegas Golden Knights vs Carolina Hurricanes
picks.rationale: The Carolina Hurricanes enter Game 1 with a dominant 12-1 postseason record and a 9-1 record in their last 10 games, including a four-game winning streak. They boast elite goaltending from Frederik Andersen, who has a 1.41 GAA and a .931 save percentage in the playoffs, and a league-best 1.62 goals against per game in the postseason. With a clean injury report and home-ice advantage, Carolina's superior defensive structure and goaltending should lead them to a victory in the series opener.

picks.game: Vegas Golden Knights vs Carolina Hurricanes
picks.rationale: Both the Golden Knights and Hurricanes are entering the Stanley Cup Final with exceptional defensive form, allowing 2.0 and 1.8 goals per game respectively over their last 10 contests. Carolina has allowed more than two goals only once in 13 playoff games, highlighting their suffocating defensive system. With both starting goaltenders, Frederik Andersen and Carter Hart, performing at Vezina-caliber levels, and Game 1s in the Stanley Cup Final often being cautious, a low-scoring affair is highly anticipated.

picks.game: Vegas Golden Knights vs Carolina Hurricanes
picks.rationale: Despite Carolina being favored, the Vegas Golden Knights have a strong track record as underdogs, covering the puck line in each of their last nine games in that role and winning five of their last five as underdogs. Historically, the Hurricanes have struggled to cover the puck line as home favorites against Pacific Division opponents, failing to do so in 12 of their last 13 games. Given that Game 1s in the finals are often tightly contested, getting +1.5 goals with Vegas provides excellent value.

picks.game: Vegas Golden Knights vs Carolina Hurricanes
picks.rationale: Carter Hart is confirmed as the starting goaltender for the Vegas Golden Knights. The Carolina Hurricanes are known for their high shot volume, averaging 33.7 shots per game in the postseason, and their relentless forecheck. Hart has been in excellent form, recording at least 29 saves in five of his last six games, and has historically performed well against the Hurricanes, stopping at least 29 shots in seven of his 11 career starts against them.

picks.game: Vegas Golden Knights vs Carolina Hurricanes
picks.rationale: Logan Stankoven is an active player for the Carolina Hurricanes, centering the productive second line alongside Taylor Hall and Jackson Blake. He has been a significant offensive contributor in the playoffs, leading the team with nine goals. Furthermore, Stankoven has demonstrated past success against the Golden Knights, recording a point in each of the two regular-season meetings between the teams.

picks.game: San Diego Padres vs Philadelphia Phillies
picks.rationale: The Philadelphia Phillies hold a significant advantage in this matchup, having swept the Padres last week and maintaining a 3-0 season record against them. Sharp money is heavily backing the Phillies, with the moneyline steaming from -125 to -145 and receiving 95% of moneyline dollars at Circa. Padres starter Randy Vasquez has a concerning 6.21 xERA, while Phillies starter Aaron Nola has been solid in his recent starts, with the Phillies going 4-1 in his last five. Both teams are coming off a day off, a situation where home favorites have a 66% win rate this season.

picks.game: Chicago White Sox vs Minnesota Twins
picks.rationale: The White Sox have a substantial pitching advantage with Davis Martin (8-1, 2.00 ERA), who has been highly reliable with the White Sox going 10-1 in his starts, including a dominant outing against these same Twins recently. Conversely, Twins starter Connor Prielipp (1-3, 5.13 ERA) was hit hard by Chicago in his last start, allowing six earned runs in 4.1 innings. Sharp money is strongly on the White Sox, with their moneyline moving from -115 to -125 and receiving 91% of moneyline dollars at DraftKings. Additionally, the White Sox offense performs well against left-handed pitching, ranking fifth in MLB in wRC+ and sporting a .780 OPS.

picks.game: Los Angeles Dodgers vs Arizona Diamondbacks
picks.rationale: Arizona presents strong value as a home underdog due to a significant pitching mismatch in their favor. Michael Soroka (7-2, 3.25 ERA) has been excellent for the Diamondbacks, while Dodgers starter Eric Lauer (2-5, 5.95 ERA) has struggled with a high ERA and xERA. The Diamondbacks won Monday's series opener 4-1, carrying recent momentum. Despite the public heavily backing the Dodgers (91-93% of public dollars), the line has barely moved, indicating sharp money coming in on Arizona (reverse line movement).

picks.game: Kansas City Royals vs Cincinnati Reds
picks.rationale: Sal Stewart of the Cincinnati Reds has significantly better numbers when facing left-handed pitchers this season, with his batting average improving from .256 to .283 and his slugging percentage from .443 to .587. Tonight, the Royals are starting a left-handed pitcher, Noah Cameron, creating a favorable matchup for Stewart. Given his strong splits against lefties and the pitching matchup, Stewart is well-positioned to achieve two or more total bases. Additionally, with Elly De La Cruz on the injured list, other Reds hitters like Stewart may see increased offensive opportunities.

picks.game: Baltimore Orioles vs Boston Red Sox
picks.rationale: The Red Sox have a favorable pitching matchup with Connelly Early (5-2, 2.95 ERA) on the mound, who recently threw seven shutout innings. The Orioles have struggled against left-handed pitching this season, holding a 6-13 record. Boston is returning home after a successful road trip, winning two of three in Cleveland. While both teams have key injuries, the Red Sox's strong pitching advantage against the Orioles' struggles versus lefties, combined with their recent form, gives them a solid edge at home.
